text: L-Argininium phosphite (LAPI), chemical formula C6H17N4O5P, molecular weight 256.20, is a nonlinear optical single-crystal salt combining the cationic L-arginine amino acid and the monoanionic phosphite (H2PO3−) counterion. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, chiral monoclinic space group P21 (No. 4), with unit cell a = 9.3993 Å, b = 4.8638 Å, c = 13.2395 Å, β = 105.249°, Z = 2, volume 583.95 Å³, determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 296 K with low R factors (R = 3.65%, wR = 9.26%, CCDC 1026960). The structure exhibits a three-dimensional hydrogen-bonded supramolecular network of L-argininium cations and phosphite anions linked by strong O—H/N—H interactions (O—H: 47.7%, H—H: 41.5%, N—H: 5.0%), forming parallel 1D chains along the crystallographic b-axis. This non-centrosymmetric crystal structure, combined with the presence of a chiral centre, gives rise to significant piezoelectric, pyroelectric, and nonlinear optical properties. DFT calculations and experimental measurements confirm a piezoelectric charge coefficient d22 of 12 pC/N along the (010) plane, comparable to quartz (2.17 pC/N) and superior to ammonia dihydrogen phosphate. Second harmonic generation (SHG) efficiency was measured at 20 mV, with standard KDP as reference—84% that of KDP. The crystal exhibits excellent mechanical strength (Vickers hardness Hv up to 9 kg/mm²), high crystalline perfection (FWHM of (200) diffraction peak: 46 arcsec), and robust laser damage thresholds (6.3 J/cm² single shot; 0.63 GW/cm² single shot; 5.5 J/cm² multiple shots; 0.55 GW/cm² multiple shots), making LAPI a promising bioorganic NLO material for photonic and optoelectronic device applications.
experimental_method: Piezoelectric coefficient measurement (silver paste electrodes); Second Harmonic Generation (Kurtz-Perry technique, standard KDP reference); Laser damage threshold (Nd:YAG, 1064 nm)
property_ref_title: An efficient piezoelectric single-crystal L-argininium phosphite: structural, Hirshfeld, electrical and mechanical analyses for NLO applications
